project.name: Cat
project.summary: TypeScript logging and observability package ecosystem for lightweight structured logging, transports, formatting, tracing, redaction, and sampling.
goals: Own the public @sylphx/cat package family, including logger APIs, formatters, transports, serializers, tracing, redaction, OTLP export, and tail-sampling behavior.
Maintain TypeScript source, package boundaries, tests, benchmarks, documentation, examples, bundle-size expectations, and npm release readiness for the Cat ecosystem.
Keep the logging surface portable across supported JavaScript runtimes without introducing product-specific application behavior.
nonGoals: Own Sylphx Platform observability infrastructure, metrics storage, tracing backends, billing, deployment, or runner policy.
Own application-specific log schemas, event taxonomies, retention policy, customer data policy, or product analytics decisions.
Own organization-wide release automation semantics outside the Cat package release workflow.

owns.name: cat-core-logger
owns.description: The core logger API, structured log record model, JSON formatting, console transport, and error serialization behavior.

owns.name: cat-extension-packages
owns.description: Optional packages for pretty formatting, file/http transports, OTLP export, trace context, redaction, and tail-based sampling.

owns.name: cat-package-docs-and-benchmarks
owns.description: Docs, examples, benchmark claims, package READMEs, and package-specific release notes for the Cat ecosystem.

doesNotOwn: Central observability infrastructure, log ingestion services, alerting products, platform SLO policy, or customer-specific logging configuration.
Reusable release tooling such as SylphxAI/bump, organization-wide Changesets policy, or GitHub runner fleet configuration.
Downstream application behavior that consumes Cat as a logging dependency.

allowedDependencies.surface: shared package release workflow invoked from .github/workflows/release.yml
project manifest schema, runner policy, CI admission doctrine, and agent execution standards

forbiddenCouplings: Do not encode downstream application-specific logging schemas, retention rules, billing semantics, or customer policy into Cat packages.
Do not make Cat depend on Sylphx Platform runtime services, deployment topology, or proprietary observability backends.
Do not change reusable release governance from this repo; package release policy belongs in the central doctrine or shared release tooling.

delivery.deployPath: Pull requests and merge groups run the repository CI workflow. Main releases use .github/workflows/release.yml, which delegates package publication to the shared SylphxAI/.github release workflow.
delivery.productionProof: Package-impacting work must prove CI, affected package tests, build output, Changesets release intent when publishing, and npm package/version readback after release.

packageRelease.releaseIntent: Changesets files in .changeset/ declare package-level release intent and changelog content.
packageRelease.versionPr: Changesets versioning prepares package version and changelog updates before publication.
packageRelease.publisher: The Release workflow delegates to SylphxAI/.github/.github/workflows/release.yml@main with inherited secrets.
packageRelease.publishProof: Release proof is the shared release workflow conclusion plus npm version readback for every affected @sylphx/cat package.

commercial.decisionHome: README.md and future ADRs for material packaging or positioning changes.
commercial.pricingSourceOfTruth: No product pricing source of truth; Cat is an open-source package ecosystem.
commercial.metricsSourceOfTruth: npm package downloads, benchmark reports, CI results, and downstream issue reports.
guardrails: Do not introduce pricing, packaging, or commercial positioning changes without ADR-backed market and usage evidence.
Benchmark and size claims must remain reproducible from repository tests or documented benchmark commands.

gaps.description: Cat uses repo-local legacy CI contexts rather than central ADR-29 admission contexts.
gaps.target: Adopt central admission workflow when the shared package template is available.

gaps.description: Cat currently uses Changesets through the shared release workflow; future doctrine should state the standard package-release path and bot ownership explicitly.
gaps.target: Package release doctrine ADR and shared workflow catalog.
